rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK, A UTAH STATE VS. CHRISTOHER C WOOLSEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER WOOLSEY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Defendant Christopher Woolsey's motion to set aside default judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant's default was entered on March 11, 2015, and a default judgment was entered on March 26, 2015. A motion to set aside a default judgment brought under Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(b) must be filed within six months. (Code Civ. Proc. § 473(b).) "The six-month limit is mandatory; a court has no authority to grant relief under section 473, subdivision (b), unless an application is made within the six-month period." (Arumbula v. Union Carbide Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 333, 340.) The Court has no authority to grant relief from the six-month limit, whether based on mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable or an attorney's affidavit of fault. (Id. at 345; accord, Austin v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 918, 918 ["The six-month time limitation is jurisdictional; the court has no power to grant relief under section 473 once the time has lapsed."].) Alternatively, a motion to set aside a default judgment may be brought within two years under Section 473.5 if the moving party establishes that service has not resulted in actual notice in time to defend the action. (Code Civ. Proc. § 473.5.) That provision does not apply. Contrary to Defendant's contention that he was never served with papers, the proof of service reflects that he was personally served with the summons and complaint on November 15, 2014, and Plaintiff's counsel's declaration establishes that Defendant was aware of the pendency of the action. In any event, the motion was not filed within two years of entry of the default judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 12, CROSS DEFENDANT C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., A CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendant C.I. Energia Solar S.A.'s ("CIE") demurrer to Bagatelos Glass Systems, Inc.'s ("BAGS") amended cross-complaint is overruled in its entirety. The breach of oral agreement cause of action alleges sufficient facts and is not uncertain. (See Amended Cross-Complaint, 8-11 and 28-29.) BAGS alleges that CIE provided non-conforming goods and services and given this stage of the litigation, CIE can reasonably respond to the amended complaint. The fraud/misrepresentation claims are well-pleaded. BAGS identifies Carlos Amin as the person who made the representations and the discovery and summary adjudication motions that the parties have already litigated ought to have cleared up any uncertainty. The trial is set to be heard in a month. The demurrer to the quantum meruit cause of action is overruled. (See Newport Harbor Ventures, LLC v. Morris Cerullo World Evangelism (2016) 6 Cal.App.5th 1207, 1223 [noting that a party can plead inconsistent theories of recovery for breach of contract and quantum meruit].) Forcing BAGS to expressly allege that it is only pursuing the quantum meruit claim if the breach of contract claim fails is busy work. The court liberally reads the complaint per Code of Civil Procedure § 452 and it is hornbook law that BAGS cannot simultaneously recover on both claims. The demurrer to the seventh cause of action for disgorgement is overruled. The scope of CIE's work is a factual issue. The demurrer to the eighth cause of action is overruled. The economic loss rule does not apply to negligently performed services. (See North American Chemical Co. v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 764, 783-784.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JEAN-YVES LENDORMY VS. KYLE HANSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Re-Scheduled Hearing Re: Motion To Correct Amend Judgment - As To Kyle Hanson Only
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JEAN-YVES LENDORMY'S Motion To Correct Amend Judgment - As To Kyle Hanson Only Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy's renewed motion to correct/amend judgment is granted. Consistent with the Court's September 21, 2018 Order Confirming Arbitration Award, which awarded costs to Plaintiff, the Court's Judgment of the same date shall be amended to award Plaintiff costs as against Kyle Hanson in the amount of $21,856.62. (Code Civ. Proc. § 473(d); People v. Landon White Bail Bonds (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 66, 69, 77 ["a judgment may be corrected at any time to reflect the trial court's manifest intent."].) rulings.Case Title: JACK H REISNER VS. UNITED BROTHERHOOD OF CARPENTERS AND JOINERS OF AM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories For Failure To Respond
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT UNITED BROTHERHOOD OF CARPENTERS NATIONAL JOB's Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories For Failure To Respond. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The responses and objections were not timely served, therefore, all objections have been waived. Plaintiff shall serve verified responses without objections by July 26, 2019 unless he files a motion for relief from failure to object in which case the responses will be due 14 days after the Court rules on that motion. Sanctions in the amount of $700 awarded to Defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA STAEDLER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Records Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JAN GUESS, ALBERT WATERS, VICKY HENNESSY, WILLIAM SCOTT, LASHAWNA BROWN'S Motion To File Records Under Seal Grant. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The Court will sign the proposed order including with the moving papers. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL KEMPEL VS. CROUSE & ASSOCIATES INSURANCE BROKERS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three; Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs Ccp 2030, 2031
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL KEMPEL's Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three; Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs Ccp 2030, 2031. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part; denied in part. As to RFPD No. 58, Defendant shall produce any reports that it ran (or can run) using software that reflects the websites that were visited by Defendant's employees. Defendant is not required to check the individual computers of each employee. Truly private and confidential information can be redacted but must be logged. If there is a dispute regarding the redactions, the reports shall be submitted to the Court for an in-camera review. The motion as to RFPD Nos. 60 and 61 is denied as the Court finds that they are overbroad and the information can be obtained in less burdensome means such as depositions or specific interrogatories. Plaintiff's request for a date certain for Defendant to complete its production of documents is granted. Defendant shall produce all responsive documents by August 12, 2019. The requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN RAMIREZ VS. J E H ENTERPRISES, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT JEH ENTERPRISES, INC. Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Defendant JEH Enterprises, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is denied. Plaintiff Christian Ramirez's complaint is a representative action containing a single cause of action in which Plaintiff is acting as a private attorney general and is seeking solely civil penalties. As such, under controlling law, Plaintiff's PAGA claims are not subject to arbitration or waiver pursuant to a predispute arbitration agreement. (Iskanian v. CLS Transportation Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal.4th 348, 382-383; Betancourt v. Prudential Overall Supply (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 439, 445-446; Esparza v. KS Industries, L.P. (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1228, 1247 ["if Employee intends to (1) limit the claims pursued to PAGA representative claims seeking civil penalties paid largely into state coffers and (2) waive the claims for individualized relief (such as the recovery of unpaid wages pursuant to Lab. Code, § 558), then the litigation can proceed on those limited claims."].) Unlike the plaintiff in Esparza, Plaintiff here has "unambiguously state[d] his intention" not to pursue private claims for victim-specific relief, such as claims to recover wages under Labor Code section 558. (Esparza, 13 Cal.App.5th at 1234.) As Defendant concedes, it disagrees with the California Supreme Court's decision in Iskanian, which is binding on this Court. (Auto Equity Sales, Inc. v. Superior Court (1962) 57 Cal.2d 450, 455.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN A. BARTHOLOMEW VS. GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTS GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC., THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY, DANIEL STONE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants Golden Gate Tow Inc., The John Stewart Company, and Daniel Ingram Stone dba Cornerstone Real Estate Services, LLC's demurrer to Plaintiff Marilyn Bartholomew's first amended complaint (FAC) is continued to July 25, 2019 for the parties' failure to comply with the meet and confer obligations imposed by Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41. Although Counsel for Defendants produced a declaration purporting to satisfy her meet and confer obligations, Plaintiff has filed a declaration denying meet and confer occurred. Rather than making judgments about which declaration is more credible, the Court concludes that the sufficiency of the meet and confer has been called into question and the parties should, irrespective of prior efforts, meet and confer in good faith to determine if they can resolve their disputes. The parties are ordered to meet and confer by phone or in person by July 18, 2019, and to inform the Court in advance of the continued hearing if Plaintiff intends to amend her complaint. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN A. BARTHOLOMEW VS. GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Golden Gate Tow Inc.'s special motion to strike the first, second, and fifth causes of action of Plaintiff Marilyn Bartholomew's first amended complaint (FAC) for fraud, conspiracy to defraud, and intentional infliction of emotional distress is denied. Golden Gate Tow has not met its burden under the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis. In order for a claim to be subject to the anti-SLAPP statute, the claim must arise from any written or oral statement or writing made in connection with an issue under consideration or review by a judicial body. (Code Civ. Proc. § 425.16(e)(2); Rand Resources, LLC v. City of Carson (2019) 6 Cal.5th 610, 623.) Where a cause of action alleges both protected and unprotected activity, the cause of action will not be subject to the anti-SLAPP statute if the protected conduct is merely incidental to the unprotected conduct. (Philipson & Simon v. Gulsvig (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 347, 358-359.) "[T]he mere fact that an action was filed after protected activity took place does not mean the action arose from that activity for the purposes of the anti-SLAPP statute." (Navellier v. Sletten (2002) 29 Cal.4th 82, 89.) Plaintiff's FAC references Small Claims Court Case No. CSM-18-857224 and the surrounding negotiations over the return of Plaintiff's towed vehicle, but these allegations are included primarily for context and need not be relied upon by the three causes of action at issue on this motion. (FAC 37, 49, 51-52.) The gravamen of these causes of action against Golden Gate Tow are seizure of Plaintiff's vehicle without a valid basis for doing so, falsely representing that the vehicle would be returned to her free of charge, and then manufacturing a parking violation that Golden Gate Tow could use to continue holding her vehicle and extract money of out her. (FAC 17, 19, 27, 30, 31, 33, 34, 36, 70-73, 76-79, 84-86, 107, 109-110; see Haight Ashbury Free Clinics, Inc. v. Happening House Ventures (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539, 1550 ["In general, whether a cause of action is subject to a motion to strike under the SLAPP statute turns on whether the gravamen of the cause of action targets protected activity."].) These allegations of fraud and infliction of emotional distress primarily relate to fraudulent activity before the small claims action was instituted or, with regard to the allegedly fraudulent invoice, after the settlement agreement was reached. (Id.) The allegations about emails providing false evidence are primarily made against Defendant Mercedes Gavin, not against Golden Gate Tow, and thus have no bearing on this analysis. (FAC 39, 40, 43, 44, 108.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
